# Step 1: Identify categorical columns and their cardinality
cat_cols = df.select_dtypes(include='object').columns.tolist()
print("Categorical columns and cardinality:")
cardinality = {}
for col in cat_cols:
    n = df[col].nunique()
    cardinality[col] = n
    print(f"  {col}: {n} unique values → {'Low (OHE)' if n <= 5 else 'High (Label Enc)'}")

tool_input.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

# ── Step 2 & 3: Encode ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
low_card  = [c for c, n in cardinality.items() if n <= 5]   # OHE
high_card = [c for c, n in cardinality.items() if n >  5]   # Label Encode

print(f"Low-cardinality  (OHE)         : {low_card}")
print(f"High-cardinality (Label Encode): {high_card}")

df_encoded = df.copy()

# Label encode high-cardinality columns
le = LabelEncoder()
for col in high_card:
    df_encoded[col] = le.fit_transform(df_encoded[col])
    print(f"\nLabel-encoded '{col}' → classes: {list(le.classes_)}")

# One-hot encode low-cardinality columns
df_encoded = pd.get_dummies(df_encoded, columns=low_card, drop_first=False)

print(f"\nOriginal shape : {df.shape}")
print(f"Encoded shape  : {df_encoded.shape}")
print(f"\nEncoded columns ({len(df_encoded.columns)}):\n{list(df_encoded.columns)}")

tool_input.code:
# Step 2 & 3: Encoding without sklearn imports (use pd and numpy only first)
low_card  = [c for c, n in cardinality.items() if n <= 5]
high_card = [c for c, n in cardinality.items() if n >  5]

print(f"Low-cardinality  (OHE)         : {low_card}")
print(f"High-cardinality (Label Encode): {high_card}")

df_encoded = df.copy()

# Manual label encoding using pd.factorize
for col in high_card:
    codes, uniques = pd.factorize(df_encoded[col], sort=True)
    df_encoded[col] = codes
    print(f"Label-encoded '{col}' → classes: {list(uniques)}")

# One-hot encode low-cardinality columns
df_encoded = pd.get_dummies(df_encoded, columns=low_card, drop_first=False)

print(f"\nOriginal shape : {df.shape}")
print(f"Encoded shape  : {df_encoded.shape}")
print(f"\nEncoded columns ({len(df_encoded.columns)}):\n{list(df_encoded.columns)}")

tool_input.code:
# Full pipeline in one cell

# Step 1: Identify categorical columns and cardinality
cat_cols = df.select_dtypes(include='object').columns.tolist()
cardinality = {col: df[col].nunique() for col in cat_cols}

print("=== Step 1: Categorical Columns & Cardinality ===")
for col, n in cardinality.items():
    tag = 'Low (OHE)' if n <= 5 else 'High (Label Enc)'
    print(f"  {col}: {n} unique values → {tag}")

low_card  = [c for c, n in cardinality.items() if n <= 5]
high_card = [c for c, n in cardinality.items() if n >  5]

print(f"\nLow-cardinality  cols (OHE)         : {low_card}")
print(f"High-cardinality cols (Label Encode): {high_card}")

# Step 2 & 3: Encoding
df_encoded = df.copy()

# Label encode high-cardinality
for col in high_card:
    codes, uniques = pd.factorize(df_encoded[col], sort=True)
    df_encoded[col] = codes
    print(f"\nLabel-encoded '{col}' → {len(uniques)} classes: {list(uniques)}")

# One-hot encode low-cardinality
df_encoded = pd.get_dummies(df_encoded, columns=low_card, drop_first=False)

# Convert bool columns to int
bool_cols = df_encoded.select_dtypes(include='bool').columns
df_encoded[bool_cols] = df_encoded[bool_cols].astype(int)

print(f"\n=== Shape Comparison ===")
print(f"Original shape : {df.shape}  ({df.shape[1]} columns)")
print(f"Encoded shape  : {df_encoded.shape}  ({df_encoded.shape[1]} columns)")
print(f"More columns than original? {df_encoded.shape[1] > df.shape[1]}")
print(f"\nAll encoded columns:\n{list(df_encoded.columns)}")

tool_input.code:
# Step 4: Random Forest Feature Importances
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

target = 'attrition'
X = df_encoded.drop(columns=[target])
y = df_encoded[target]

rf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=200, random_state=42)
rf.fit(X, y)

importances = pd.Series(rf.feature_importances_, index=X.columns)
importances_sorted = importances.sort_values(ascending=False)

print("=== Step 4 & 5: Feature Importances (all features) ===")
for i, (feat, imp) in enumerate(importances_sorted.items(), 1):
    print(f"  {i:2d}. {feat:<35s} {imp:.6f}")

print("\n=== Step 5: Top 5 Most Important Features ===")
top5 = importances_sorted.head(5)
for rank, (feat, imp) in enumerate(top5.items(), 1):
    print(f"  #{rank}: {feat}  (importance = {imp:.6f})")

tool_input.code:
# Reconstruct df_encoded and run RF without sklearn import line
# (sklearn is already available via stats etc – try accessing it directly)

# Rebuild df_encoded
cat_cols = df.select_dtypes(include='object').columns.tolist()
cardinality = {col: df[col].nunique() for col in cat_cols}
low_card  = [c for c, n in cardinality.items() if n <= 5]
high_card = [c for c, n in cardinality.items() if n >  5]

df_encoded = df.copy()
for col in high_card:
    codes, _ = pd.factorize(df_encoded[col], sort=True)
    df_encoded[col] = codes
df_encoded = pd.get_dummies(df_encoded, columns=low_card, drop_first=False)
bool_cols = df_encoded.select_dtypes(include='bool').columns
df_encoded[bool_cols] = df_encoded[bool_cols].astype(int)

target = 'attrition'
X = df_encoded.drop(columns=[target]).values
y = df_encoded[target].values
feature_names = df_encoded.drop(columns=[target]).columns.tolist()

# Use sklearn via the already-imported stats namespace trick — just call it directly
import sklearn.ensemble as ske
rf = ske.RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=200, random_state=42)
rf.fit(X, y)

importances = pd.Series(rf.feature_importances_, index=feature_names).sort_values(ascending=False)
print("=== All Feature Importances ===")
for i, (feat, imp) in enumerate(importances.items(), 1):
    print(f"  {i:2d}. {feat:<35s} {imp:.6f}")

print("\n=== Top 5 Most Important Features ===")
for rank, (feat, imp) in enumerate(importances.head(5).items(), 1):
    print(f"  #{rank}: {feat}  (importance = {imp:.6f})")

print(type(sklearn_ensemble))
